mission.title: Install and Configure Schemahero on Kubernetes
mission.description: SchemaHero is a Kubernetes operator designed for declarative database schema management, allowing users to manage database schemas as Kubernetes resources. Installing SchemaHero simplifies the process of deploying and altering database schemas in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Install the SchemaHero kubectl plugin
steps.description:
Download and install the SchemaHero kubectl plugin using the following commands. Replace `<OS>` and `<ARCH>` with your operating system and architecture (e.g., linux and amd64). For example, for Linux amd64:
```bash
curl -LO https://github.com/schemahero/schemahero/releases/download/v0.23.3/kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz
sudo tar -xzf kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin
rm kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the SchemaHero plugin is installed correctly by running:
```bash
kubectl schemahero version
```

steps.title: Create a SchemaHero namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for SchemaHero:
```bash
kubectl create namespace schemahero
```

steps.title: Deploy SchemaHero operator
steps.description:
Apply the SchemaHero operator manifest:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/schemahero/schemahero/main/deploy/schemahero-operator.yaml --namespace schemahero
```

steps.title: Verify the operator is running
steps.description:
Ensure that the SchemaHero operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n schemahero
```

steps.title: Configure RBAC for SchemaHero
steps.description:
Set up necessary RBAC permissions for SchemaHero to manage resources:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/schemahero/schemahero/main/deploy/rbac.yaml --namespace schemahero
```

uninstall.title: Remove SchemaHero operator
uninstall.description:
Delete the SchemaHero operator resources:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/schemahero/schemahero/main/deploy/schemahero-operator.yaml --namespace schemahero
```

uninstall.title: Delete the SchemaHero namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the namespace created for SchemaHero:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace schemahero
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current SchemaHero resources for backup:
```bash
kubectl get all -n schemahero -o yaml > schemahero-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the SchemaHero plugin
upgrade.description:
Download the latest version of the SchemaHero kubectl plugin:
```bash
curl -LO https://github.com/schemahero/schemahero/releases/download/v0.23.3/kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz
sudo tar -xzf kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin
rm kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the version to confirm the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl schemahero version
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator pod not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the SchemaHero operator pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n schemahero
``` If there are errors related to RBAC, ensure that RBAC permissions are correctly set.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl schemahero command not found
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that the kubectl plugin is installed correctly and is in your PATH. Verify installation with:
```bash
which kubectl-schemahero
```

troubleshooting.title: Schema changes not applying
troubleshooting.description:
Check the status of the SchemaHero custom resources:
```bash
kubectl get schemahero -n schemahero
``` If the status is not 'Ready', check the operator logs for errors.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SchemaHero will have the operator running in the 'schemahero' namespace, and the kubectl plugin will be available for managing database schemas. You can verify the operator's health and functionality through the provided commands.
codeSnippets:
curl -LO https://github.com/schemahero/schemahero/releases/download/v0.23.3/kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz
sudo tar -xzf kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin
rm kubectl-schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed.
